questions.question: An agent's latency varies from 3 to 30 seconds and logs only show the LLM calls. Why is end-to-end tracing the fix?
options: Tracing makes the agent faster automatically
One trace spans the agent loop, LLM calls, tool dispatch, MCP round-trips, and sub-agents — exposing things like an MCP server hanging on cold-start
Tracing replaces the need for logs
Providers refund slow requests if you have traces
questions.explanation: The February 2026 debugging story in the lesson: the cause was a cold-start hang in one MCP server — invisible in LLM logs, obvious in a span hierarchy where every hop nests under one trace id.

questions.question: Why do the OTel GenAI semantic conventions matter, rather than custom span attributes?
options: Custom attributes are rejected by collectors
Stable shared attribute names mean Datadog, Langfuse, Phoenix, OpenLLMetry, and AgentOps all parse the same spans — instrument once, ship to any backend
The conventions are required by law in the EU
They compress spans better
questions.explanation: The semconv group standardized gen_ai.* attribute names (stable from v1.37). All backends speak OTLP and parse the same fields, so instrumentation isn't coupled to an observability vendor.

questions.question: Which attributes does an LLM span require under the GenAI conventions?
options: Only the model name
gen_ai.operation.name, provider.name, request.model, response.model, usage input/output tokens, and response.id
The full prompt and completion text
CPU and memory usage
questions.explanation: Operation, provider, requested vs served model, token usage, and the provider response id for correlation. Tool spans add gen_ai.tool.name and tool.call.id; agent spans add gen_ai.agent.* fields.

questions.question: How do the conventions treat prompt and completion content by default?
options: Captured in full on every span
Off by default — spans carry metrics and timing; content capture is opt-in via env vars because payloads are large and may contain PII
Captured but automatically encrypted
Only the first 100 characters are kept
questions.explanation: Privacy-safe defaults: timing and token counts, no content. Enabling gen_ai.content.* events requires explicit opt-in (OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN plus content-capture vars) and deserves careful review before production.

questions.question: When is a span marked SpanKind.CLIENT vs INTERNAL?
options: CLIENT for user-facing agents, INTERNAL for background jobs
CLIENT when crossing a process boundary (LLM provider, MCP server); INTERNAL for the agent's own loop steps and tool execution
CLIENT for errors, INTERNAL for successes
The kind is assigned randomly by the SDK
questions.explanation: Span kind encodes the call topology: llm.chat and mcp.call are CLIENT spans (remote hops), while agent.invoke_agent and tool.execute are INTERNAL — making the hierarchy agent → LLM → tool → MCP readable in any trace viewer.
